result:
All confirmed directly against source (cbm's graph query returned empty for `struct rq` fields/edges, so this answer relies on grep/read verification, not the graph).

## Per-runqueue lock field

`kernel/sched/sched.h:964`
```c
struct rq {
	/* runqueue lock: */
	raw_spinlock_t		__lock;
```
(struct `rq` starts at `sched.h:962`; comment at 963; field `__lock` at 964.)

## Not plain `spin_lock` ŌĆö v6.6 uses an indirection layer

Since core scheduling (`CONFIG_SCHED_CORE`) landed, code must never touch `rq->__lock` directly. Two accessor helpers pick the actual lock (a CPU's own `__lock`, or when core scheduling is enabled, the shared `rq->core->__lock` for all siblings on that core):

- `rq_lockp()` ŌĆö `kernel/sched/sched.h:1235` (CONFIG_SCHED_CORE) / `kernel/sched/sched.h:1339` (non-CONFIG_SCHED_CORE fallback)
- `__rq_lockp()` ŌĆö `kernel/sched/sched.h:1243` / `kernel/sched/sched.h:1343`

Primary take/release helpers (declared `sched.h:1366-1368`, defined `kernel/sched/core.c`):

| Function | Definition |
|---|---|
| `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(rq, subclass)` | `kernel/sched/core.c:551` |
| `raw_spin_rq_trylock(rq)` | `kernel/sched/core.c:576` |
| `raw_spin_rq_unlock(rq)` | `kernel/sched/core.c:600` |

`raw_spin_rq_lock(rq)` (`sched.h:1370`) is a thin inline wrapping `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(rq, 0)`. `_irq`/`_irqsave` variants (`sched.h:1375-1403`) layer on top of these.

Internally, `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested()` retries `raw_spin_lock_nested(__rq_lockp(rq), subclass)` in a loop, re-checking `__rq_lockp(rq)` didn't change (the core-cookie can be re-pointed mid-lock when core scheduling toggles) ŌĆö this is the "may differ from older kernels" part: pre-core-scheduling kernels just did `raw_spin_lock(&rq->lock)` directly.

## Two real acquisition call sites in `kernel/sched/core.c`

- `kernel/sched/core.c:616` ŌĆö `double_rq_lock()`: `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq1);`
- `kernel/sched/core.c:636` ŌĆö `__task_rq_lock()`: `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq);`

(also present at `core.c:660` in `task_rq_lock()`, `core.c:2689` in `push_cpu_stop()`, `core.c:9264`/`9498`/`9516` ŌĆö listed in case you want alternates.)
